title: glaucium grandiflorum subsp. refractum
intro: Glaucium grandiflorum subsp. refractum, also known as the lesser cornflower or purple poppy, is a biennial plant species within the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and alleviating muscle spasms. In herbal medicine, Glaucium grandiflorum subsp. refractum can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or extract, and is often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, bronchitis, and menstrual cramps.
